Verify Agent TARS report against cloned source code

Major corrections:
- Context Engineering: L0-L3 tiers NOT found in source; rewritten to
  reflect actual WorkspacePack/ContextReferenceProcessor/ImageProcessor
- Event Stream: added verified type names (AgentEventStreamProcessor,
  CoreEventType with actual event names)
- Browser strategies: added verified class names (BrowserHybridStrategy,
  BrowserVisualGroundingStrategy, BrowserDOMStrategy)
- Inheritance chain: verified Agent → MCPAgent → AgentTARS
- gui-agent/: expanded from 3 to 8 packages (added agent-sdk, cli,
  operator-nutjs, operator-adb, operator-aio)
- omni-tars/: expanded to 5 sub-packages (core, omni-agent, mcp-agent,
  gui-agent, code-agent)
- Build instructions: multimodal uses pdk not turbo; corrected to
  pnpm bootstrap + pnpm dev workflow
- Added Node.js version inconsistency note (root >=20, multimodal >=22)
- Updated dependency graph with @tarko/agent-snapshot, @agent-tars/interface
- MCP SDK version corrected to ^1.12.1 in mcp-agent
- Updated confidence assessment with source-verified ratings

commit.message:
feat: az-diagram-autogen v0.1.0

Interactive Azure architecture diagram generator with 605 official Azure icons.

- CLI: az-diagram-autogen -s services.json -c connections.json -o arch.html
- Python API: from az_diagram_autogen import generate_diagram
- 30+ Azure service types with auto-categorization
- Drag-and-drop, pan/zoom, VNet boundaries, PE visualization
- Multi-subscription/RG hierarchy support
- Self-contained single HTML output
- 17 unit tests, 3 example JSON files

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add Agent TARS architecture deep analysis report

Release v0.1.26

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: unify Jira + SNow bookmarklets into one 'Toolbox Relay'

The relay script already auto-detects Jira vs SNow via location.hostname —
there was never a technical reason for two separate bookmarklets.

- Renamed all bookmarklet links to '📎 Toolbox Relay'
- SNow Step 1 in CRG and PRB settings now points to the Jira bookmarklet
  instead of providing a duplicate drag target
- Removed cr-snow-bm and prb-snow-bm anchor elements from the DOM
- Removed dead references from crInitBookmarklets() and prbInitBookmarklets()
- tbxUpdateDragHints() simplified — no more separate snow hint IDs
- All Step 3 text updated to reflect unified bookmark name

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(cli): swallow terminal spawn errors gracefully — P8 smoke test passes

- openTerminal() now attaches error handler instead of crashing on ENOENT
- Warp/wt missing on non-Windows prints warning, does not exit non-zero
- P8 verified: Linear fetch → worktree → CLAUDE.md → agent spawn → log ✅

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: Cerebro
repository.description: Cerebro is an Agent Orchestrator manages a fleet of specialized sub-agents, tools, and models to solve complex, multi-step problems. It specializes in decomposition, delegation, and synthesis

commit.message:
Release v0.1.25

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: PRB Generator — full symmetric partial-create recovery

Handles all 4 partial-success scenarios:
1. Both succeed         → full create + link (unchanged)
2. Defect ✓ Story ✗    → show 'Retry Story Only' button pre-filled with defect key
3. Story  ✓ Defect ✗   → show 'Retry Defect Only' button pre-filled with story key
4. Both already exist   → guard: error if both existing keys are filled

'Existing Defect' and 'Existing Story' input fields on the Create form:
- Fill one to skip that issue's creation and go straight to create+link
- Clear both for normal full creation flow
- Button label updates to reflect mode (Defect Only / Story Only / Both)
- prbRetryStoryOnly() mirrors prbRetryDefectOnly() symmetrically

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Translate README to English

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Initial commit: add README and Flutter app variants (codex + claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: PRB auto-fix string→option format error + Retry Defect Only button

prbCreateIssue() now handles two auto-fix types in one retry pass:
- 'not on the appropriate screen' → field removed from payload
- 'no String-argument constructor from String value' → field auto-converted
  from plain string to {value: string} (fixes customfield_10253 = 'Production')

prbRetryDefectOnly(storyKey): new function for the partial-success case
where story was created but defect failed. Shows 'Retry Defect Only' button
pre-filled with the existing story key — creates only the defect and links it.

PRB._lastCreate stores {defectPayload, storyPayload, linkType, jiraUrl} so
retry has full context without re-fetching.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(cli): build cerebro CLI — enables Phase 8 smoke test

Commands:
  cerebro start                  — poll Linear, auto-spawn agents
  cerebro spawn <proj> <issue>   — spawn agent for specific issue
  cerebro status                 — list active sessions
  cerebro send <session> <msg>   — queue message to running agent

Core:
  - config.ts: loads agent-orchestrator.yaml, walks up to find repo root
  - session-store.ts: persists sessions + assignment-log.jsonl
  - linear-tracker.ts: @linear/sdk wrapper (getIssue, listOpen, comment)
  - orchestrator.ts: model routing, conductor spec/plan, CLAUDE.md + opencode.jsonc
    writer, git worktree creation, agent + terminal spawner, memory/working update

Binary linked globally as 'cerebro'

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Release v0.1.24

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: PRB Generator — correct Jira field format for option, priority, issuelink

prbFormatFieldValue corrections:
- option:    {name:val} -> {value:val}  (Jira custom selects use 'value', not 'name')
- priority:  numeric values now sent as {id:val} instead of {name:val}
             (stored value '4' is a Jira ID, not a priority name)
- issuelink: {key:val} -> plain string  (Epic Link / Feature Key expects bare key)
- array:     items now {value:} instead of {name:} for multi-select custom fields

prbInferSchemaType() helper:
- If Jira reports schema.type='string' but field has allowedValues, infer 'option'
  (fixes customfield_10253 and similar misdetected fields on future imports)
- Detects epic-link custom fields and maps to 'issuelink'

Action required for existing templates:
- customfield_10253 stored as 'string' type needs to be changed to 'Option'
  in Field Builder > Settings (one-time fix for existing saved templates)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Release v0.1.23

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: PRB auto-strip screen-incompatible fields and retry

When Jira rejects fields with 'not on the appropriate screen', automatically
strip those fields and retry the create — fully transparent to the user.

On success, shows which fields were auto-removed (e.g. customfield_10265,
customfield_10254 on Defect; customfield_10253 on Story).
On retry failure, debugBlock shows the stripped payload actually sent.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Release v0.1.22

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: replace 'while' with 'whilst' in README prose

Apply British English conventions throughout human-readable
documentation. The conjunction 'whilst' is preferred over 'while'
in formal British prose.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: PRB Generator — robust error display with full Jira response

- Rewrote prbCreate() promise chain: defect and story errors captured independently
  (previously, defect failure would shadow story error or swallow it entirely)
- Added 'Show Jira response' debug collapse alongside payload debug in error display
- New captureError() preserves e.responseData from relay error objects
- Added console.log for all payloads sent and responses received
- console.error with full response data on any Jira API rejection
- Error display now shows both the formatted error AND the raw Jira response body
  so users can see exactly WHY their payload was rejected

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add initial web UI structure and README for RF/CAM Sweep Tool (Updated) (#23)

* Add initial HTML structure for web UI tool

Checked `dvntone/wscanplus_desktop` as the safer target instead of `wscanplus` due to the one-open-PR rule.

Current blocker:
- repo is visible
- write operations from the current GitHub integration are blocked
- creating a feature branch returned 403: Resource not accessible by integration

Recommended repo structure for the Android/no-build web version:
  /claude/webui_tool/
    index.html
    app.js
    README.md

This keeps it static-host friendly and avoids Node/build-tool setup.

Add TextEntity for high-level text rendering and demos (#22)

* Phase 3: TextEntity – high-level text rendering entity

Add TextEntity (inherits SpriteEntity) that wraps TextRenderer with a
dirty-flag pattern: property setters (setText, setFont, setTrueTypeFont,
setStyle) mark dirty, and update() lazily rebuilds the backing texture.

New files:
  - include/vde/api/TextEntity.h, src/api/TextEntity.cpp
  - tests/TextEntity_test.cpp (10 tests)
  - examples/mission_control_demo (real-time text-heavy demo)
  - smoketests/scripts/smoke_mission_control_demo.vdescript

Changes:
  - GameAPI.h: added TextEntity.h umbrella include
  - GameTypes.h: added Color::operator==
  - TextRenderer.h: added TextStyle::operator==
  - vertical_shooter: replaced manual createTextTexture()/font table
    with TextEntity (-104 lines), removed Sprites text helpers
  - CMakeLists.txt, examples/CMakeLists.txt, tests/CMakeLists.txt,
    scripts/smoke-test.ps1: registered new sources and smoke mapping

* Add Text Adventure demo and associated assets; implement smoke tests

* Refactor example scenes to remove hardcoded auto-terminate durations; implement CLI --timeout support for dynamic termination

* Fix four review issues: TextEntity attach, timeout parsing, scripted char input, missing cctype (#23)

* Initial plan

* Fix review comments: TextEntity onAttach, parseTimeoutArg safety, printable ASCII keys, cctype include

Co-authored-by: derekdk <3893574+derekdk@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix pelican landing page links for print/single-file targets (#1128)

* Initial plan

* Fix: print targets get better links in pelican generated site

When output_filename is not set for single-file output formats (PDF, EPUB,
KINDLE, BRAILLE), deploy_path() now scans the output directory for the
actual output file and returns a path including the filename. This allows
the pelican-generated landing page to link directly to the PDF (or other
single-file output) rather than the directory containing it.

feat: animated drag hints, auto-retry, last-connected timestamps, advanced options

- Animated bookmarklet drag guidance with pulsing arrow on all 5 bookmarklet links
- Auto-retry (tbxRetryOnce): on relay-closed/timeout, silently reconnects + retries once
- Last connected timestamp in connection bar, refreshed every 30s
- Advanced Power User collapsible sections in CRG/PRB/MI settings (PAT, proxy, Edge docs)

commit.message:
feat(api): enforce squad number immutability on PUT (#529)

Add a mismatch guard in put_async: if squad_number in the request
body does not match the path parameter, return HTTP 400 Bad Request.
The path parameter is the authoritative source of identity on PUT.

Document the single-model design decision in PlayerRequestModel:
one model intentionally covers both POST and PUT, with per-operation
differences handled at the route layer.

commit.message:
Fix pelican landing page links for print/single-file targets (#1128)

* Initial plan

* Fix: print targets get better links in pelican generated site

When output_filename is not set for single-file output formats (PDF, EPUB,
KINDLE, BRAILLE), deploy_path() now scans the output directory for the
actual output file and returns a path including the filename. This allows
the pelican-generated landing page to link directly to the PDF (or other
single-file output) rather than the directory containing it.

commit.message:
feat: add download-index command for pre-built index from GitHub Releases

Users can now get semantic search in ~30 seconds instead of 15-30 minutes:
  npx @jasonbellz/gospel-library-mcp download-index

Downloads index.db from the latest GitHub Release and saves it to
~/.gospel-library-mcp/index.db. Uses atomic write (.tmp + rename)
to avoid corruption if interrupted.

Also:
- Export DB_DIR from vectorStore.ts (needed by downloader)
- Update startup/search fallback messages to suggest download-index first
- Update build-index message to reflect title+description indexing

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add top-level README pointer

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add contextual Tips & Tricks section for remaining daily protein goal (#7)

Users had no in-app guidance on how to close the gap between their
current intake and daily protein goal. This adds a dynamic tips section
that surfaces food suggestions calibrated to the remaining amount.

### Changes

- **`index.html`** — New `#tips-section` container placed below Today's
Log; hidden by default via `display:none`
- **`app.js`**
  - `getTips(remaining)` — returns 3 contextual tips across 4 tiers:
    | Remaining | Suggestions |
    |-----------|-------------|
    | ≥ 60g | Chicken breast 200g, tuna + shake combo, meal planning |
    | 30–59g | Whey shake, tuna 100g, Greek yogurt + cottage cheese |
    | 10–29g | Greek yogurt 150g, two eggs, cottage cheese 100g |
    | < 10g | Milk 200ml, one egg, cottage cheese 50g |
- `renderTips()` — shows/hides section based on remaining; hidden once
goal is met
- `render()` now calls `renderTips()` so tips update live on every entry
change
- **`style.css`** — `.tip-item` uses a green left-border accent
(`border-left: 3px solid var(--green-500)`) and reuses existing
surface/text-muted variables; consistent with the log item design

verbliebenen Rest

commit.message:
feat: index title + og:description for richer semantic embeddings

Previously only the article title was embedded. Now each document is
indexed as 'Title. Description' using the og:description meta tag,
which contains a meaningful summary of the article content.

This significantly improves semantic search recall — queries about
concepts mentioned only in the article body (not the title) now match
correctly. Existing indexes should be rebuilt with build-index.

Also includes entity decoding for meta tag content (& etc.) and
expanded Range header from 8KB to 16KB to capture longer <head> sections.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

  existing user-configured config.gs if present

Use /^\s*[^/\s].*key:/m instead of /^\s*key:/m to explicitly exclude
lines starting with comment markers (//) from the negative assertions

* fix(deploy): address security and UX issues from code review

- EC3: Use optional chaining (?.) in collectGmailEntries / collectCalendarEntries

- EC5: Show user-visible error in handleAddConfigRow when labels/calendars empty
- EC6: Reset configRowCounter at start of renderConfigSection
- EC8: Warn in addCalendarConfigRow when previously saved calendar not found
- P1:  Add post-save verification chain (verify write, check script accessible,
       show trigger status from localStorage, celebratory 🎉 UI when all green)
- P3:  Add 'Go to Step 2 to re-deploy' CTA button in error state of renderConfigSection
- P5:  Replace inline onclick with addEventListener wired after DOM insertion
- P7:  Guard handleSaveConfig against concurrent saves via data-saving attribute
- P8:  Use consistent safeId (validated scriptId) for all DOM ID lookups
- P12: Add role=status aria-live=polite to save-status spans; add aria attributes
       to config-area container
- P13: Detect 403 scope errors in handleSaveConfig and show re-auth guidance
- handlePickDoc/Folder/Sheet: use getToken() helper for fresh token before picker

* fix(deploy): fix 7 failing Playwright tests in PR #49 (#67)

- parseCalendarConfig/parseGmailConfig: strip comment lines before
  running the block regex so commented-out example blocks (which use
  single-quoted values) no longer cause a null return for empty configs

- handleSaveConfig: remove the extra GET-verify and GET-project-meta
  steps that were not mocked by the new tests; save status now shows
  "Configuration saved" directly after a successful PUT

- renderConfigSection 404 handling: change error message from
  "…could not be found — it may have been deleted or moved." to
  "…could not be found. Use 'Show deployment options' above to

* chore(ci): apply prettier/eslint auto-fixes

* fix(deploy): address remaining code review issues

- Add null guards to picker callbacks to silently discard selection if
  row was removed before the picker resolved
- Add user-visible warning when existing config.gs cannot be parsed
  (previously only a console.warn was emitted)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore(ci): apply prettier/eslint auto-fixes

* feat(deploy): 3-step save verification, celebratory UX, and focus management

Replace the simple "Configuration saved" message with a progressive
3-step verification sequence after save:
  1. Configuration saved ✅ (re-read project to confirm)
  2. Trigger active ✅ / ⚠️ inconclusive (check deployments API)
  3. Script accessible ✅ (verify project metadata)

Add celebratory confirmation UX with variant text:
  - Fresh deploy: "🎉 Your automation is live!"
  - Edit/re-save: "🎉 Your automation is updated and running."

Add focus management (WCAG 2.1 AA):
  - Focus moves to save-status after save completes
  - Focus moves to error message on config load failure
  - Focus moves to first form element after panel load

Fix parseGmailConfig/parseCalendarConfig returning null for empty
configs by skipping non-config brace blocks (function body braces).

5 new Playwright tests covering verification sequence, celebratory
text variants, trigger degradation, focus management, and edit-mode
detection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore(ci): apply prettier/eslint auto-fixes

* fix(deploy): deduplicate localStorage entries per script in Step 4

Users who deployed the same script multiple times (e.g. with different
titles) had duplicate entries in localStorage, causing Step 4 to render
multiple panels per script — some pointing at stale/deleted project IDs
that returned 404 errors.

getDeployedProjects() now deduplicates by catalogId, keeping only the
last entry per script. Adds a Playwright test confirming 3 localStorage
entries for 2 distinct scripts produce exactly 2 config panels.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Add Playwright test for deployed badge visibility

repository.name: q2
repository.description: Quaternary Quantization

commit.message:
SIMD-accelerate fp32 quantisation path and add q2_lee_distance (#72)

* Initial plan

* SIMD-accelerate fp32 quantisation path and add q2_lee_distance

- Add 128-bit SIMD (v128) fast path for dtype=0 (fp32) in q2_quantise:
  • v128.load for 4× f32 bulk loads
  • f32x4.mul/add for vectorised L2 norm accumulation
  • i8x16.shuffle for horizontal f32x4 reduction
  • f32x4.gt + v128.not + v128.bitselect for branchless symbol classification
  • v128.xor + i32x4.shr_u for vectorised Gray encoding
  • i32x4.extract_lane for direct byte packing (no pre-zeroing needed)
- Add new exported q2_lee_distance function (DESIGN.md §2.6–§2.7):
  • v128.xor + i8x16.popcnt for SIMD Hamming distance on Gray codes
  • i16x8/i32x4.extadd_pairwise for hierarchical sum reduction
  • Scalar tail loop for sub-16-byte remainders
- Update q2.ts: Q2Kernel.leeDistance interface + q2LeeDistanceDirect fallback
- Add 6 new tests for q2LeeDistanceDirect (identical, complement, adjacent,
  cyclic-wrap, symmetry, empty vectors)
- All 257 tests pass, typecheck clean, WASM validated across n=128..4096

Co-authored-by: devlux76 <86517969+devlux76@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/devlux76/q2/sessions/7c4e359f-3a13-46a7-8a66-a9e387936697

* Address code review: improve test comments for clarity

Co-authored-by: devlux76 <86517969+devlux76@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/devlux76/q2/sessions/7c4e359f-3a13-46a7-8a66-a9e387936697

* Address PR review: f32x4.le for NaN consistency, deduplicate Lee distance, fix test name

- Replace v128.not(f32x4.gt(...)) with f32x4.le(...) in SIMD path to
  match scalar f32.le NaN→D semantics (NaN comparisons return false
  with both le and gt, so le gives correct mask directly)
- Remove duplicate q2LeeDistanceDirect implementation; re-export
  leeDistancePacked from q2stats.ts to avoid divergence
- Rename complement test to clarify it returns sum over dimensions
- Update test calls to match new (a, b) signature (no n parameter)

Co-authored-by: devlux76 <86517969+devlux76@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/devlux76/q2/sessions/6a9ae6ac-eb19-4555-a97e-4760c53b5299

---------
